Home / Services / Retaining Walls

Retaining Walls

Stabilize your landscape and add visual appeal with our expertly built retaining walls.

Full Care Inc. specializes in building durable and aesthetically pleasing retaining walls that solve erosion problems and enhance your property's curb appeal. We understand the unique soil conditions in Indianapolis, where clay soil and heavy rainfall can create significant challenges. Our retaining walls are engineered to withstand these conditions, providing long-lasting support and stability.

We offer a variety of retaining wall materials, including concrete blocks, natural stone, and timber. Each material has its own unique look and benefits, and we can help you choose the right option for your specific needs and budget. Our experienced team ensures proper drainage and backfilling to prevent water damage and ensure the wall's structural integrity.

Whether you need a retaining wall to level a sloped yard, prevent soil erosion, or create a raised garden bed, Full Care Inc. has the expertise to deliver exceptional results. We take pride in our craftsmanship and attention to detail, ensuring that your retaining wall is both functional and beautiful.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Site Assessment and Design
  2. 02 Step 2: Excavation and Foundation Preparation
  3. 03 Step 3: Wall Construction and Drainage Installation
  4. 04 Step 4: Backfilling and Compaction

Benefits

  • Erosion Control and Soil Stabilization
  • Enhanced Landscape Aesthetics
  • Increased Property Value
  • Improved Usability of Sloped Yards

Frequently Asked Questions

What materials are best for retaining walls in Indianapolis?

Concrete blocks and natural stone are both excellent choices, known for their durability and resistance to the elements. Timber retaining walls are a less expensive option but may require more maintenance.

How high can a retaining wall be without a permit?

The height limit for retaining walls without a permit varies by city. Contact your local building department for specific regulations.

How important is drainage for a retaining wall?

Proper drainage is crucial for the longevity of a retaining wall. Without adequate drainage, water pressure can build up behind the wall, leading to cracks and eventual failure.
